class="h-3 w-3" />On this page

<div>

- <a href="roadmap.html#sep-prioritization"
  class="break-words py-1 block font-medium hover:text-gray-900 dark:text-gray-400 dark:hover:text-gray-300">SEP
  Prioritization</a>
- <a href="roadmap.html#priority-areas"
  class="break-words py-1 block font-medium hover:text-gray-900 dark:text-gray-400 dark:hover:text-gray-300">Priority
  Areas</a>
- <a href="roadmap.html#1-transport-evolution-and-scalability"
  class="group flex items-start break-words py-1 whitespace-pre-wrap text-gray-500 hover:text-gray-900 dark:text-gray-400 dark:hover:text-gray-300"
  style="padding-left:1rem">1. Transport Evolution and Scalability</a>
- <a href="roadmap.html#2-agent-communication"
  class="group flex items-start break-words py-1 whitespace-pre-wrap text-gray-500 hover:text-gray-900 dark:text-gray-400 dark:hover:text-gray-300"
  style="padding-left:1rem">2. Agent Communication</a>
- <a href="roadmap.html#3-governance-maturation"
  class="group flex items-start break-words py-1 whitespace-pre-wrap text-gray-500 hover:text-gray-900 dark:text-gray-400 dark:hover:text-gray-300"
  style="padding-left:1rem">3. Governance Maturation</a>
- <a href="roadmap.html#4-enterprise-readiness"
  class="group flex items-start break-words py-1 whitespace-pre-wrap text-gray-500 hover:text-gray-900 dark:text-gray-400 dark:hover:text-gray-300"
  style="padding-left:1rem">4. Enterprise Readiness</a>
- <a href="roadmap.html#on-the-horizon"
  class="break-words py-1 block font-medium hover:text-gray-900 dark:text-gray-400 dark:hover:text-gray-300">On
  the Horizon</a>
- <a href="roadmap.html#validation"
  class="break-words py-1 block font-medium hover:text-gray-900 dark:text-gray-400 dark:hover:text-gray-300">Validation</a>
- <a href="roadmap.html#get-involved"
  class="break-words py-1 block font-medium hover:text-gray-900 dark:text-gray-400 dark:hover:text-gray-300">Get
  Involved</a>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div id="content-area"
class="relative grow box-border flex-col w-full mx-auto px-1 lg:pl-[23.7rem] lg:-ml-12 xl:w-[calc(100%-28rem)]">

<div id="header" class="relative leading-none">

<div class="mt-0.5 space-y-2.5">

<div class="eyebrow h-5 text-primary dark:text-primary-light text-sm font-semibold">

Roadmap

</div>

<div class="flex flex-col sm:flex-row items-start sm:items-center relative gap-2 min-w-0">

# Roadmap

<div id="page-context-menu"
class="items-center shrink-0 min-w-[156px] justify-end ml-auto sm:flex hidden">

<div class="flex items-center gap-2 text-sm text-center font-medium">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="size-4" />Copy page

</div>

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="transition-transform text-gray-400 overflow-visible group-hover:text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-600 dark:group-hover:text-gray-400 rotate-90" />

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="mt-2 text-lg prose prose-gray dark:prose-invert [&>*]:[overflow-wrap:anywhere]">

Our plans for evolving Model Context Protocol

</div>

<div id="page-context-menu"
class="flex items-center shrink-0 min-w-[156px] mt-3 sm:hidden">

<div class="flex items-center gap-2 text-sm text-center font-medium">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="size-4" />Copy page

</div>

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="transition-transform text-gray-400 overflow-visible group-hover:text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-600 dark:group-hover:text-gray-400 rotate-90" />

</div>

</div>

<div id="content"
class="mdx-content @container/columns-container relative mt-8 prose prose-gray dark:prose-invert [contain:inline-size] isolate empty:hidden mb-14"
page-title="Roadmap" page-href="/development/roadmap">

<div class="callout my-4 px-5 py-4 overflow-hidden rounded-2xl flex gap-3 border border-neutral-200 bg-neutral-50 dark:border-neutral-700 dark:bg-white/10"
callout-type="info">

<div class="mt-0.5 w-4" component-part="callout-icon">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="flex-none size-5 text-neutral-800 dark:text-neutral-300" />

</div>

<div class="text-sm prose dark:prose-invert min-w-0 w-full [&_kbd]:bg-background-light dark:[&_kbd]:bg-background-dark [&_code]:!text-current [&_kbd]:!text-current [&_a]:!text-current [&_a]:border-current [&_strong]:!text-current text-neutral-800 dark:text-neutral-300"
component-part="callout-content">

Last updated: **2026-03-05**

</div>

</div>

<span data-as="p">This page describes our strategic priorities and what
we expect **Working Groups** and **Interest Groups** to deliver against
them.</span>

<div class="callout my-4 px-5 py-4 overflow-hidden rounded-2xl flex gap-3 border border-blue-200 bg-blue-50 dark:border-blue-900 dark:bg-blue-600/20"
callout-type="note">

<div class="mt-0.5 w-4" component-part="callout-icon">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="size-4 text-blue-800 dark:text-blue-300" />

</div>

<div class="text-sm prose dark:prose-invert min-w-0 w-full [&_kbd]:bg-background-light dark:[&_kbd]:bg-background-dark [&_code]:!text-current [&_kbd]:!text-current [&_a]:!text-current [&_a]:border-current [&_strong]:!text-current text-blue-800 dark:text-blue-300"
component-part="callout-content">

<span data-as="p">The ideas presented here are not commitments. We may
solve these challenges differently than described. Some items may not
materialize at all. This is also not an *exhaustive* list. We may
incorporate work that isn’t mentioned here.</span>

</div>

</div>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="roadmap.html#sep-prioritization"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">SEP Prioritization</span>

<span data-as="p">**SEPs that fall within the priority areas below will
receive expedited review and have the highest chance of acceptance.**
SEPs outside these areas are not automatically rejected, but
contributors should expect longer review timelines and a higher bar for
justification. Maintainer capacity is finite. We direct it toward these
priorities first.</span> <span data-as="p">If you are considering a SEP,
check whether it aligns with one of the areas below, discuss it in the
relevant
<a href="../community/working-interest-groups.html" class="link">Working
Group or Interest Group</a>, and bring that group’s backing with you.
SEPs with WG support and a clear connection to the roadmap move fastest.
See the <a href="../community/sep-guidelines.html" class="link">SEP
guidelines</a> for the full process.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="roadmap.html#priority-areas"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Priority Areas</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="roadmap.html#1-transport-evolution-and-scalability"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">1. Transport Evolution and
Scalability</span>

<span data-as="p">Streamable HTTP gave MCP a production-ready transport,
but running it at scale has revealed gaps around horizontal scaling,
stateless operation, and middleware patterns.</span>
<span data-as="p">**What we want to achieve:**</span>

- **Next-generation transport**: evolve Streamable HTTP to run
  statelessly across multiple server instances and behave correctly
  behind load balancers and proxies.
- **Scalable session handling**: define how sessions are created,
  resumed, and migrated so that server restarts and scale-out events are
  transparent to connected clients.
- **MCP Server Cards**: a standard for exposing structured server
  metadata via a `.well-known` URL, so browsers, crawlers, and
  registries can discover a server’s capabilities without connecting to
  it.

<span data-as="p">**Working Group ownership:**</span>

- **Transports WG** owns the transport and session work: a series of
  SEPs covering the wire format, session model, and resumption protocol,
  plus conformance guidance for SDK authors.
- **Server Card WG** owns the Server Card format and its distribution,
  coordinating with the broader industry AI-catalog effort.

<span data-as="p">We will **not** be introducing additional official
transports this cycle. Keeping the set small protects ecosystem
compatibility; the community should experiment via custom
transports.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="roadmap.html#2-agent-communication"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">2. Agent Communication</span>

<span data-as="p">The Tasks primitive (<a
href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol/issues/1686"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">SEP-1686</a>) gave agents
a reliable call-now / fetch-later pattern. Running it in production has
surfaced gaps in the lifecycle semantics that the **Agents WG** should
close:</span>

- **Retry semantics**: what happens when a task fails transiently, and
  who decides whether to retry.
- **Expiry policies**: how long results are retained after completion,
  and how clients learn a result has expired.

<span data-as="p">These are the gaps we can point to today. The Agents
WG should also collect and triage operational issues from production
deployments—this list will grow as more of the ecosystem runs Tasks at
scale.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="roadmap.html#3-governance-maturation"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">3. Governance Maturation</span>

<span data-as="p">MCP has grown into a multi-company open standard under
the Linux Foundation. <a
href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol/issues/1302"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">SEP-1302</a> formalized
Working Groups and Interest Groups, and <a
href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol/issues/2085"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">SEP-2085</a> established
succession and amendment procedures. The next step is giving the
community a clear path to leadership so the project does not depend on a
small set of individuals.</span> <span data-as="p">The **Governance WG**
should deliver:</span>

- **A Contributor Ladder SEP** defining the progression from community
  participant → WG contributor → WG facilitator → lead maintainer → core
  maintainer, with explicit nomination and review criteria at each step.
- **A delegation model** allowing WGs with a proven track record to
  accept SEPs and publish extension updates within their domain without
  a full core-maintainer review cycle.
- **A charter template** that every WG and IG maintains publicly: scope,
  active deliverables, success criteria, and retirement conditions,
  reviewed quarterly.

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="roadmap.html#4-enterprise-readiness"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">4. Enterprise Readiness</span>

<span data-as="p">Enterprises are deploying MCP at scale and hitting
gaps the protocol does not yet address.</span> <span data-as="p">Areas
where we need clear problem statements and directional proposals:</span>

- **Audit trails and observability**: end-to-end visibility into what a
  client requested and what a server did, in a form enterprises can feed
  into their existing logging and compliance pipelines.
- **Enterprise-managed auth**: paved paths away from static client
  secrets and toward SSO-integrated flows
  (<a href="https://xaa.dev" class="link" target="_blank"
  rel="noreferrer">Cross-App Access</a>), so IT can manage MCP access
  the same way they manage everything else.
- **Gateway and proxy patterns**: well-defined behavior when a client
  does not connect directly to a server but routes through an
  intermediary. This may include authorization propagation, session
  semantics, and what the gateway is allowed to see.
- **Configuration portability**: a way to configure a server once and
  have that configuration work across different MCP clients.

<span data-as="p">We expect an **Enterprise WG** to form to own this.
Much of the output will likely land as extensions rather than core
specification changes.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="roadmap.html#on-the-horizon"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">On the Horizon</span>

<span data-as="p">These areas have community interest and interest from
core maintainers but are not top priorities. We will support a
community-formed Working Group in any of them and review SEPs on these
topics if time permits.</span>

- **Triggers and Event-Driven Updates** — clients currently learn about
  server-side state changes by polling or holding an SSE connection
  open. A standardized callback mechanism (webhooks or similar) would
  let servers proactively notify clients when new data is available,
  with defined ordering guarantees across all transports.
- **Result Type Improvements** — tool calls, resource reads, and task
  results all arrive complete and inline. Streamed results would let
  clients receive output incrementally for interactive scenarios
  (generated text, audio, video frames); reference-based results would
  let clients decide when to pull large payloads into context rather
  than polluting it by default. This is cross-cutting: streaming touches
  transport, references touch the schema.
- **Security & Authorization** — finer-grained least-privilege scopes,
  clearer guidance on avoiding OAuth mix-up attacks, secure credential
  management on both client and server, and a community-driven
  vulnerability disclosure program routed through the Linux Foundation.
  Sponsored work is already underway: <a
  href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol/pull/1932"
  class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">SEP-1932 (DPoP)</a> and
  <a
  href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol/pull/1933"
  class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">SEP-1933 (Workload
  Identity Federation)</a>.
- **Extensions Ecosystem** — the `ext-auth` and `ext-apps` tracks are
  early proof that the extension mechanism works. Maturing them,
  investigating a Skills primitive for composed capabilities, and adding
  first-class extension support to the registry would all strengthen the
  path from experiment to standard.

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="roadmap.html#validation"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Validation</span>

<span data-as="p">A protocol specification is only as good as the
implementations that follow it. Alongside the areas above, we continue
to invest in:</span>

- **Conformance Test Suites**: automated verification that clients,
  servers, and SDKs correctly implement the specification, with coverage
  expanding alongside each new feature area.
- **SDK Tiers**: the tiering system introduced in <a
  href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol/issues/1730"
  class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">SEP-1730</a> gives
  developers a clear signal of which SDKs track the specification most
  closely.
- **Reference Implementations**: canonical implementations of new
  features to anchor community development and unblock early adopters.

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="roadmap.html#get-involved"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Get Involved</span>

<span data-as="p">MCP’s roadmap is built by its community:</span>

- **Join a Working Group or Interest Group**: see the
  <a href="../community/working-interest-groups.html" class="link">Working
  Groups &amp; Interest Groups</a> page and the
  <a href="../community/communication.html" class="link">community
  communication channels</a> to connect with the groups active in each
  area above.
- **Propose or comment on SEPs**: review the
  <a href="../community/sep-guidelines.html" class="link">SEP
  guidelines</a> and open or weigh in on proposals.
- **Start an experimental extension**: <a
  href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol/issues/2133"
  class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">SEP-2133</a> lets any WG
  or IG experiment in an `experimental-ext-` repository before a formal
  SEP is required.
- **Contribute to the project**: read the
  <a href="../community/contributing.html" class="link">contributing
  guide</a> for how to get involved with the specification, SDKs, and
  tooling.

</div>

<div class="feedback-toolbar pb-16 w-full flex flex-col gap-y-8">

<div class="flex flex-row flex-wrap gap-4 items-center justify-between">

class="fill-current" /><span class="small">Yes</span>

src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="fill-current" /><span class="small">No</span>

</div>

</div>

</div>

class="h-1.5 stroke-gray-400 overflow-visible group-hover:stroke-gray-600 dark:group-hover:stroke-gray-300" /><span

and Events Charter</span></a>

</div>

aria-hidden="true">

<div class="flex flex-col w-full rounded-2xl pointer-events-auto bg-background-light/90 dark:bg-background-dark/90 backdrop-blur-xl border border-gray-200 dark:border-white/30 focus-within:border-primary dark:focus-within:border-primary-light transition-colors cursor-pointer">

src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="lucide lucide-arrow-up text-white dark:text-white size-3.5" />

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="flex gap-6 flex-wrap">

<a href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol" class="h-fit"
target="_blank"><span class="sr-only">github</span><img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-5 h-5 bg-gray-400 dark:bg-gray-500 hover:bg-gray-500 dark:hover:bg-gray-400" /></a>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>
